topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The attic air hung thick and dusty, smelling of aged paper and forgotten things. Liam was rummaging through boxes of his late grandmother’s belongings, intending only to find some old photographs. He pulled out a shoebox labeled ‘School Years.’ Inside, nestled beneath yellowed permission slips and dried flowers, was a stack of notebooks. His breath hitched in his throat as he flipped through them. Every single assignment, from rudimentary geometry proofs to elaborate historical essays, was meticulously recorded. He stopped, his fingers trembling slightly as he traced the neat handwriting detailing his entire middle school career. A slow, disbelieving smile spread across his face, and he let the stack slide back into the box, his eyes wide as saucers.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" Sarah muttered, her voice barely a whisper as she stared at the mountain of graded papers spread across the kitchen table. Her mother, Clara, had always been a whirlwind of forgetfulness, leaving papers scattered or, worse, lost entirely. But here, organized by subject, dated precisely, was a complete chronicle of Sarah’s academic life. She picked up a crumpled worksheet from sophomore year, the ink still sharp. A sudden, almost shaky laugh escaped her lips. She ran a hand through her hair, her gaze darting between the papers and her mother, who was currently engrossed in knitting. "Mom," Sarah began, her tone laced with incredulity, "did you... keep all of this?"

[story 3]

Mark found the file tucked deep inside his mother's seldom-used desk drawer. It wasn't a photo album or a collection of recipes; it was a binder, spine cracked with age, filled wall-to-wall with every test, every project, every homework assignment from his primary school through high school graduation. Mark sat down heavily on the floor, the sheer volume of documented effort overwhelming him. He lifted a particularly detailed diagram from the binder, his fingers brushing against the crisp paper. He stared at the evidence of years of diligent work, a profound stillness settling over him. He blinked several times, trying to process the reality of the meticulously preserved academic history resting in his mother's possession.
